title: Electra mutica
intro: Electra mutica, also known as sea heath or sea heather, is a type of shrub native to coastal areas of the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and needle-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Electra mutica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Electra mutica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, particularly skin and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Electra mutica is a species of desert grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant is classified as a perennial grass that is native to the deserts of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the genus Electra, which is a small group of grasses that are primarily found in the American Southwest. Electra mutica is often used as a reference species in studies of desert grassland ecology due to its wide distribution and adaptability. The classification of Electra mutica reflects its unique characteristics and its ability to thrive in arid environments.
